title: Lush Spanish Danmuji Paella
description: A funky, molten Valencian paella featuring danmuji and lamination-puff rice, cooked on a hot salt block for a monsoon‑like finish.
instructions: Preheat the salt block to 180°C and lightly oil it with 1 tbsp olive oil.
Heat the salt block in a large pan over medium heat, then sauté diced onion and minced garlic until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Add sliced red bell pepper and smoked tofu cubes, cooking for 2 minutes until heated through.
Stir in 1 tsp smoked paprika, cooking for 30 seconds to release aromas.
Add 200g short-grain rice, stirring to coat grains with oil and spices, and cook for 2 minutes.
Pour in 1 liter vegetable broth, bring to a gentle boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer.
Add 100g diced danmuji, 50g puffed rice, and 50g frozen peas, covering loosely and simmering until rice is tender, 15 to 18 minutes.
Finish with 1 tbsp lemon zest, season with salt to taste, and garnish with extra puffed rice for texture.
Let the paella rest on the salt block for 5 minutes before serving for a molten, monsoon finish.
